name: Norway

colors.symbolism: from the Dannebrog; recalls the union with Denmark
from the Dannebrog; together with red and blue evokes the tricolours of liberty
freedom and independence; drawn from the French and American flags as emblems of liberty

description: A red field with a blue Nordic cross fimbriated white; the vertical arm is shifted toward the hoist.
history: Designed by Storting member Fredrik Meltzer and adopted by the Norwegian parliament on 13 July 1821, during the union with Sweden, as a distinct merchant ensign. Norway was however required to fly the union mark (the 'sildesalat') in the canton until 1898 (merchant) and 1905 (state); the plain cross flag became the sole national flag with the dissolution of the union with Sweden on 7 June 1905. The design self-consciously combines the red-and-white of Denmark (with which Norway had been in union 1380–1814) and the blue of liberty associated with the post-1789 republican tradition.

features.symbolism: the off-centre Nordic cross expresses Christian heritage and kinship with the other Nordic nations; the colour scheme combines the red and white of the Danish Dannebrog (recalling the centuries-long Dano-Norwegian union) with the blue of liberty drawn from the French and American tricolours
